commit.message:
Replace unverified claims in MCP vs Skills comparison with factual ones

Removed:
- "200-2000ms per lookup" — no MCP-specific latency benchmarks exist
- "~60% token overhead" — fabricated percentage; real overhead varies
- "One page at a time" — MCP servers can return multiple pages
- "100% reliable" — overstated
- "Zero overhead" — still costs tokens to read files
- "<1ms" — technically correct but cited without source

Replaced with verifiable claims:
- "Network round-trip per lookup" (true by definition)
- "Sub-millisecond local file reads" (verified: NVMe reads are 20-170µs)
- "Lower token cost — no protocol wrapping per call" (factual)
- "No extra dependencies at query time" (factual)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add OpenTelemetry traces and metrics hooks (#89)

## Summary

Adds a new `openfeature-otel-hook` gem under `hooks/` providing two
OpenTelemetry hooks for the OpenFeature Ruby SDK:

- **TracesHook** — adds `feature_flag.evaluation` span events to the
active OTel span in the `finally` hook stage
- **MetricsHook** — tracks four evaluation counters (`active_count`,
`requests_total`, `success_total`, `error_total`) across the full hook
lifecycle

Both hooks consume the core SDK's `Telemetry.create_evaluation_event`
utility (shipped in open-feature/ruby-sdk#240) and depend only on
`opentelemetry-api` — applications bring their own OTel SDK and
exporters.

### Why `hooks/` instead of `providers/`

This establishes a new top-level `hooks/` directory parallel to
`providers/`, matching the [Go SDK
contrib](https://github.com/open-feature/go-sdk-contrib/tree/main/hooks/open-telemetry)
convention. Hooks and providers serve different purposes and belong in
separate namespaces.

### Spec compliance

- [OTel Semantic Conventions for Feature Flag
Events](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/feature-flags/feature-flags-events/)
— the hooks remap `error.message` → `feature_flag.error.message` per the
events spec
- [OpenFeature Spec Appendix D
(Observability)](https://openfeature.dev/specification/appendix-d/)

## Test plan

- [x] 17 RSpec examples (5 traces + 12 metrics), all passing
- [x] 100% line coverage
- [x] `bundle exec rubocop` clean
- [x] CI job added for Ruby 3.4 + head
- [x] Release Please configured at v0.1.0

*🤖 Jose's AI agent*

---------

Signed-off-by: Jose Colella <jose.colella@gusto.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(validate): walk up path to find .attractor ancestor in validateCwd

The previous fix only stripped .attractor when it was the immediate
parent of the dotfile. DAGs nested at .attractor/pipelines/deep/
had basename="deep", so stripping never fired, causing a
false-positive prompt_file_not_found warning.

Now uses lastIndexOf(".attractor") on the split path to strip the
entire .attractor/... suffix regardless of nesting depth.

Adds a regression test covering the nested-subdirectory case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(assets): add 10 classic Hammond B-3 engine presets (11-20)

Historically researched registrations from legendary organists:

11 Jon Lord Hard Rock — 888 833 100, Leslie slow+OD 0.95 (Marshall
   stack simulation, Deep Purple "Smoke on the Water" style)
12 Gregg Allman Southern Rock — 888 660 000, C3, 3rd-harmonic perc,
   fast Leslie, OD 0.4 (Allman Brothers documented setting)
13 Larry Young Modal Jazz — 008 620 000, V1, 2nd perc slow, clean
   (Blue Note "Unity" 1965, post-bop modal approach)
14 Shirley Scott Bebop — 808 400 000, V1, 3rd perc slow, no OD
   ("Queen of the Organ", no 5⅓' = cleaner less-growly bebop tone)
15 Rod Argent Time of the Season — 008 076 000, V2, 2nd perc slow,
   no OD (upper-partial emphasis, Zombies 1968 distinctive nasal tone)
16 Wild Bill Davis Cocktail — 038 640 000, C1, no perc, slow Leslie
   (pioneer organ-trio cocktail lounge warmth, swing-to-R&B bridge)
17 Jack McDuff Hard Funk — 888 200 000, C2, 2nd perc fast, OD 0.2
   ("Rock Candy" / "Honeydripper" staccato funk, prominent key click)
18 Chester Thompson R&B Groove — 866 680 000, C3, 2nd perc fast,
   OD 0.35 (Tower of Power, complex midrange R&B groove chunk)
19 Billy Preston Gospel Rock — 808 880 000, C3, 2nd perc fast, OD 0.3
   (no 5⅓' = churchy not growly, Beatles "Get Back" gospel-rock)
20 Jimmy McGriff Soul Jazz — 868 600 006, C3, 2nd perc fast, OD 0.35
   (documented 868600006 setting, 1' at 6 sparkle through overdrive)

Total Hammond presets: 20

Defer Resend client construction to avoid crash without API key

and any environment without RESEND_API_KEY. Lazy-init the client
so it only constructs when actually sending email.

commit.message:
feat: reskin 4 games, add 3 new genres, rebuild Arc, create brand doc

Narrative reskins (mechanics unchanged, story aligned to Substrate):
- Airlock → Memory Management Protocol (purge/preserve data, OOM killed)
- Bootloader → System Initialization Protocol (watchdog timer, boot stages)
- Chemistry → Synthesis (combine AI capabilities: compute, data, logic)
- Tactics → Agent Deployment Protocol (Root/Sentinel/Pixel/Spec vs threats)

New games (3 missing popular genres filled):
- IDLE (Substrate Growth) — incremental/clicker, 8 generators, 20 upgrades
- DECKBUILDER (Stack Overflow) — Slay the Spire style, 13 cards, 9 enemies, 3 bosses
- RUNNER (Pipeline) — endless runner, 5 obstacle types, parallax backgrounds

Arc agent rebuilt — now tests HTML validity, JS syntax, mobile readiness,
asset integrity, internal links, and file sizes (was only checking file existence)

Arcade portal reorganized — Games vs Tools sections, Snatcher/Idle/Deckbuilder/Runner added

Brand document created at memory/brand.md — voice, visual identity, messaging framework

fix: defer GSAP import to browser to prevent SSR 500 error

serverless SSR. Dynamic import behind $app/environment browser guard
fixes the issue with scrollIntoView fallback until GSAP loads.

commit.message:
Optimize collections and JSON parsing performance

- Vector/CompareVector: in-place shift on Remove instead of allocating new array
- Vector/CompareVector: increase growth factor from 25% to 50% for fewer reallocations
- Heap: use Runtime->Copy instead of element-by-element loop in Resize
- Map.Filter: use GetKeyValues() to avoid redundant O(log n) Find() lookups
- JSON scanner: direct char comparison for true/false/null keywords, skip String allocation

commit.message:
fix: enforce seniority checks in initial scoring, not just deep analysis

- Add post-panda seniority level-gap correction (0-5 granular scale)
  - Gap ≥3: 0.45x penalty (exec/senior → entry/open)
  - Gap ≥2 (known level): 0.55x penalty
  - Gap ≥2 (unknown level): 0.70x penalty (ambiguous jobs)
  - Gap ≤-2: 0.50x underqualified penalty
  - Gap ≤-3: 0.35x severely underqualified penalty
- HIGH-CONFIDENCE FLOOR now disabled for seniority-mismatched jobs
  (was protecting local≥80 from LLM correction even when overqualified)
- LLM seniority weight increased 20% → 30% with explicit rules:
  "jobs without Senior/Lead/Principal → score ≤40 for senior candidates"
- Applied same correction to fallback scoring path

Fix auto-queuing: use HTTP POST to /prompt API instead of broken PromptServer methods

The previous queue_helper used non-existent PromptServer methods

only the first job to run. Now POSTs to ComfyUI's /prompt endpoint,
which is the standard programmatic queuing mechanism.

fix: add LLM-based location verification and protest actor role semantics

GDELT frequently geo-tags events to countries merely mentioned in articles
rather than where events actually occurred (e.g., an LA protest tagged to
Lebanon). This adds:

- LocationVerification output schema to canonical interpretation — LLM now
  verifies whether GDELT's coordinates match the actual event location
- Location mismatches flagged in raw_data["location_mismatch"] for downstream
  correction
- "demonstrator" actor role for protests (protesters are never "perpetrators")
- Protest-specific role guidance in interpretation prompt
- Moved backfill_interpretation from genai.py to backfill.py (proper home)

commit.message:
Strip null bytes from feed and full-text content before storing

PostgreSQL rejects text containing 0x00 even though SQLite accepts it
silently. Readability-fetched content from some sites includes null
bytes, causing INSERT failures logged as 'invalid byte sequence for
encoding UTF8: 0x00'.

Add sanitizeText() to the feeds package (strips null bytes and invalid
UTF-8) and apply it to all content fields in StoreArticles and both
store calls in FetchFullTextForArticles.

commit.message:
Fix whitelist response shape, alerts null crash, and advancements import resilience

- AdminController.GetWhitelist: return PaginatedResponse shape ({ data, page, pageSize, totalCount })
  instead of { players } so WhitelistPage can read data?.data correctly
- DbSeeder: seed TpsAlertThreshold = 15f so alert config is never NULL in a fresh DB
- AlertConfigPage: merge API data with DEFAULT_CONFIG on load to guard against null numeric
  fields (null value → controlled input crash)
- HistoricalImportService Phase 3: wrap per-player advancement processing in try/catch so a
  single bad file doesn't abort all remaining players; also use ValueKind check instead of
  GetBoolean() to avoid InvalidOperationException on non-boolean done fields
